  1807	# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS
  1808	
  1809	## Focus
  1810	FINAL SYNTHESIS: I consolidated the repeated iteration themes into 10 non-overlapping decisions so every substantive Mnemosyne finding lands in one bucket: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.
  1811	
  1812	## Findings
  1813	### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade for Public
  1814	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:106](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L106), [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [memory-search.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [memory-search.ts:809](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L809); evidence: both
  1815	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper forwards search requests to a backend whose BM25/vector/RRF internals are only README-documented, while Public already runs a visible multi-stage hybrid search pipeline.
  1816	- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable value is wrapper ergonomics, not ranking replacement, so using Mnemosyne as a reason to re-architect Public retrieval would be a category error.
  1817	- **Recommendation**: reject
  1818	- **Impact**: high
  1819	
  1820	### Finding 2: Compaction-time memory action guidance is the strongest adopt-now idea
  1821	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:210](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [opencode-transport.ts:142](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L142), [memory-surface.ts:640](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/memory-surface.ts#L640); evidence: both
  1822	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne preserves memory behavior across compaction with an explicit action card, while Public currently preserves continuity/state but not equally direct memory-tool verbs on the OpenCode surface.
  1823	- **Why it matters for us**: A transport-owned, deduped memory reminder would improve post-compaction behavior without changing retrieval authority.
  1824	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  1825	- **Impact**: high
  1826	
  1827	### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s startup/AGENTS discipline should be reused only after translation into Public terms
  1828	- **Source**: [external/README.md:64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L64), [context-server.ts:755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [session-bootstrap.ts:163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [memory-save.ts:1136](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1136); evidence: both
  1829	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” in AGENTS and compaction, but Public’s save/delete paths are governed and more constrained.
  1830	- **Why it matters for us**: The behavioral pattern is good, but the wording must point to `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, governed saves, and careful deletions instead of cheap direct CLI mutations.
  1831	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  1832	- **Impact**: medium
  1833	
  1834	### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping
  1835	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L118), [external/src/index.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L160), [memory-search.ts:529](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L529), [memory-search.ts:818](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L818); evidence: wrapper-confirmed
  1836	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
  1837	- **Why it matters for us**: Thin read aliases could improve ergonomics, but only if they compile down to existing scope logic and do not invent new authority.
  1838	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  1839	- **Impact**: medium
  1840	
  1841	### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
  1842	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/src/index.ts:79](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L79), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [memory-save.ts:1265](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1265); evidence: both
  1843	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne normalizes the current directory basename into a collection key and auto-inits that collection on load.
  1844	- **Why it matters for us**: That is fragile across renames, sibling worktrees, and same-name repos, and it is materially weaker than Public’s governed scope model.
  1845	- **Recommendation**: reject
  1846	- **Impact**: high
  1847	
  1848	### Finding 6: An ergonomic inline memory intake is valid only as a governed facade
  1849	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [generate-context.js:85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [memory-save.ts:1128](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1128), [memory-save.ts:1273](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1273); evidence: both
  1850	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne lets the agent store concise strings directly, while Public’s durable save path is JSON-primary, file-backed, preflighted, and governed.
  1851	- **Why it matters for us**: A `memory_remember`-style helper could be useful, but only if it produces the same governed save artifacts rather than bypassing them.
  1852	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
  1853	- **Impact**: medium
  1854	
  1855	### Finding 7: A direct `core=true` shortcut should not be added to Public’s main surface
  1856	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/README.md:57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L57), [vector-index-store.ts:609](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts#L609), [memory-crud-update.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L67); evidence: both
  1857	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a simple `core` tag, while Public reserves that behavior for explicit constitutional-tier handling and budgeted cache/query logic.
  1858	- **Why it matters for us**: A shortcut would blur `constitutional`, `critical`, and ordinary memories and encourage over-promotion.
  1859	- **Recommendation**: reject
  1860	- **Impact**: medium
  1861	
  1862	### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
  1863	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138); evidence: both
  1864	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes append-plus-delete behavior at the wrapper boundary, while Public wraps updates/deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, checkpointing, and hook invalidation.
  1865	- **Why it matters for us**: Public should keep its auditable mutation model and treat contradiction cleanup as structured revision, not as plain-text ID deletion.
  1866	- **Recommendation**: reject
  1867	- **Impact**: high
  1868	
  1869	### Finding 9: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
  1870	- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:330](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L330), [opencode-transport.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L104); evidence: both
  1871	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shows that a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel good, and Public already emits OpenCode transport plans from MCP-owned state.
  1872	- **Why it matters for us**: If we build a native facade, it must delegate to MCP for save/search/recovery and keep CocoIndex/code-graph as separate retrieval planes, or it will become a second authority layer.
  1873	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
  1874	- **Impact**: medium
  1875	
  1876	### Finding 10: Optional offline backend readiness is worth prototyping, but Mnemosyne’s runtime stack should not become Public’s default
  1877	- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751), [session-bootstrap.ts:194](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L194); evidence: both
  1878	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary plus a first-run model download and mainly reports failures through stderr or one install hint, while Public already has explicit bootstrap/readiness surfaces.
  1879	- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
  1880	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  1881	- **Impact**: medium
  1882

  1898	## Assessment
  1899	- Executive summary: 10 consolidated decisions. `adopt now`: 2. `prototype later`: 2. `NEW FEATURE`: 2. `reject`: 4. The highest-confidence transfers are compaction-time memory action guidance and rewritten startup policy; the highest-confidence rejections are basename-derived scope, `core=true`, and Mnemosyne-style mutation semantics.
  1900	- New information ratio: 0.46
  1901	- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
  1902	- Questions answered: Public should borrow Mnemosyne’s behavioral ergonomics, not its authority model. The safe path is `session_bootstrap`-aligned guidance plus a transport-owned compaction memory card, with any future aliases or facades compiled into existing MCP/governance surfaces.
  1903	- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, plus `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted` from the read-only sandbox.
  1904	
  1905	## Reflection
  1906	- What worked: source-first tracing from [`external/src/index.ts`](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts) into Public’s transport, bootstrap, save, and mutation handlers made the true boundary obvious: Mnemosyne contributes behavior cues and wrapper ergonomics, not a stronger core memory substrate.
  1907	- What did not work: the external repo exposes no Go backend internals beyond README claims, the phase folder still lacks a canonical `research/research.md`, and the read-only sandbox prevented updating phase docs or saving phase memory.
  1908	
  1909	## Recommended Next Focus
  1910	Design two packet-ready follow-ons before any runtime edits: one for a deduped OpenCode compaction memory action card owned by transport, and one for a governed `memory_remember` / read-only `project|global` facade that delegates entirely to existing MCP scope and save authority.
  1911	
  1912	
  1913	hook: Stop

  1916	90,595
  1917	# Iteration 020: FINAL SYNTHESIS
  1918	
  1919	## Focus
  1920	FINAL SYNTHESIS: I consolidated the repeated iteration themes into 10 non-overlapping decisions so every substantive Mnemosyne finding lands in one bucket: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, `NEW FEATURE`, or `reject`.
  1921	
  1922	## Findings
  1923	### Finding 1: Mnemosyne is not a retrieval-engine upgrade for Public
  1924	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:106](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L106), [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [memory-search.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [memory-search.ts:809](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L809); evidence: both
  1925	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne’s wrapper forwards search requests to a backend whose BM25/vector/RRF internals are only README-documented, while Public already runs a visible multi-stage hybrid search pipeline.
  1926	- **Why it matters for us**: The transferable value is wrapper ergonomics, not ranking replacement, so using Mnemosyne as a reason to re-architect Public retrieval would be a category error.
  1927	- **Recommendation**: reject
  1928	- **Impact**: high
  1929	
  1930	### Finding 2: Compaction-time memory action guidance is the strongest adopt-now idea
  1931	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:210](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js:396](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js#L396), [opencode-transport.ts:142](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L142), [memory-surface.ts:640](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/hooks/memory-surface.ts#L640); evidence: both
  1932	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne preserves memory behavior across compaction with an explicit action card, while Public currently preserves continuity/state but not equally direct memory-tool verbs on the OpenCode surface.
  1933	- **Why it matters for us**: A transport-owned, deduped memory reminder would improve post-compaction behavior without changing retrieval authority.
  1934	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  1935	- **Impact**: high
  1936	
  1937	### Finding 3: Mnemosyne’s startup/AGENTS discipline should be reused only after translation into Public terms
  1938	- **Source**: [external/README.md:64](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L64), [context-server.ts:755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [session-bootstrap.ts:163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163), [memory-save.ts:1136](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1136); evidence: both
  1939	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions” in AGENTS and compaction, but Public’s save/delete paths are governed and more constrained.
  1940	- **Why it matters for us**: The behavioral pattern is good, but the wording must point to `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, governed saves, and careful deletions instead of cheap direct CLI mutations.
  1941	- **Recommendation**: adopt now
  1942	- **Impact**: medium
  1943	
  1944	### Finding 4: Read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases are worth prototyping
  1945	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L118), [external/src/index.ts:160](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L160), [memory-search.ts:529](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L529), [memory-search.ts:818](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L818); evidence: wrapper-confirmed
  1946	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne gives simple project/global tool pairs; Public already has richer runtime scoping via `retrievalLevel` and broader fallback behavior.
  1947	- **Why it matters for us**: Thin read aliases could improve ergonomics, but only if they compile down to existing scope logic and do not invent new authority.
  1948	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  1949	- **Impact**: medium
  1950	
  1951	### Finding 5: Basename-derived project identity is too weak for Public’s memory authority
  1952	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:28](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L28), [external/src/index.ts:79](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L79), [external/README.md:55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L55), [memory-save.ts:1265](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1265); evidence: both

  1954	- **Why it matters for us**: That is fragile across renames, sibling worktrees, and same-name repos, and it is materially weaker than Public’s governed scope model.
  1955	- **Recommendation**: reject
  1956	- **Impact**: high
  1957

  1961	- **Why it matters for us**: A `memory_remember`-style helper could be useful, but only if it produces the same governed save artifacts rather than bypassing them.

  1972	### Finding 8: Mnemosyne’s blind append and hard-delete lifecycle is the wrong primary mutation model for Public
  1973	- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138); evidence: both

  1978	
  1979	### Finding 9: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
  1980	- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:330](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L330), [opencode-transport.ts:104](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L104); evidence: both
  1981	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne shows that a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel good, and Public already emits OpenCode transport plans from MCP-owned state.
  1982	- **Why it matters for us**: If we build a native facade, it must delegate to MCP for save/search/recovery and keep CocoIndex/code-graph as separate retrieval planes, or it will become a second authority layer.
  1983	- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
  1984	- **Impact**: medium
  1985	
  1986	### Finding 10: Optional offline backend readiness is worth prototyping, but Mnemosyne’s runtime stack should not become Public’s default
  1987	- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751), [session-bootstrap.ts:194](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L194); evidence: both
  1988	- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary plus a first-run model download and mainly reports failures through stderr or one install hint, while Public already has explicit bootstrap/readiness surfaces.
  1989	- **Why it matters for us**: If we ever support optional local memory backends, they need a real warmup/doctor/readiness flow; Public should not switch its primary path to Bun-plus-binary-plus-model-download.
  1990	- **Recommendation**: prototype later
  1991	- **Impact**: medium
  1992

  2007	
  2008	## Assessment
  2009	- Executive summary: 10 consolidated decisions. `adopt now`: 2. `prototype later`: 2. `NEW FEATURE`: 2. `reject`: 4. The highest-confidence transfers are compaction-time memory action guidance and rewritten startup policy; the highest-confidence rejections are basename-derived scope, `core=true`, and Mnemosyne-style mutation semantics.
  2010	- New information ratio: 0.46
  2011	- Questions addressed: hybrid contract vs wrapper proof, compaction resilience, AGENTS/startup reuse, project/global ergonomics, scope identity stability, inline save ergonomics, constitutional pinning semantics, mutation/audit model, OpenCode facade design, federated retrieval separation, offline readiness tradeoffs
  2012	- Questions answered: Public should borrow Mnemosyne’s behavioral ergonomics, not its authority model. The safe path is `session_bootstrap`-aligned guidance plus a transport-owned compaction memory card, with any future aliases or facades compiled into existing MCP/governance surfaces.
  2013	- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, plus `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted` from the read-only sandbox.
  2014	
  2015	## Reflection
  2016	- What worked: source-first tracing from [`external/src/index.ts`](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts) into Public’s transport, bootstrap, save, and mutation handlers made the true boundary obvious: Mnemosyne contributes behavior cues and wrapper ergonomics, not a stronger core memory substrate.
  2017	- What did not work: the external repo exposes no Go backend internals beyond README claims, the phase folder still lacks a canonical `research/research.md`, and the read-only sandbox prevented updating phase docs or saving phase memory.
  2018	
  2019	## Recommended Next Focus
  2020	Design two packet-ready follow-ons before any runtime edits: one for a deduped OpenCode compaction memory action card owned by transport, and one for a governed `memory_remember` / read-only `project|global` facade that delegates entirely to existing MCP scope and save authority.
  2021

   142	
   143	function buildStructuralRoutingNudge(
   144	  structuralContext: StructuralBootstrapContract,
   145	): SessionBootstrapResult['structuralRoutingNudge'] | null {
   146	  if (structuralContext.status !== 'ready') {
   147	    return null;
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  return {
   151	    advisory: true,
   152	    readiness: 'ready',
   153	    preferredTool: 'code_graph_query',
   154	    message: 'Advisory only: when the next question is about callers, imports, dependencies, or outline, prefer `code_graph_query` before Grep or Glob.',
   155	    preservesAuthority: 'session_bootstrap',
   156	  };
   157	}

   159	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   160	   3. HANDLER
   161	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   162	
   163	/** Handle session_bootstrap tool call — one-call session setup */
   164	export async function handleSessionBootstrap(args: SessionBootstrapArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   165	  const startMs = Date.now();
   166	  const allHints: string[] = [];
   167	
   168	  // Sub-call 1: session_resume with full resume payload
   169	  let resumeData: Record<string, unknown> = {};
   170	  try {
   171	    const resumeResponse = await handleSessionResume({
   172	      specFolder: args.specFolder,
   173	    });
   174	    resumeData = extractData(resumeResponse);
   175	    allHints.push(...extractHints(resumeData));
   176	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   177	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   178	    resumeData = { error: message };
   179	    allHints.push('session_resume failed. Try calling it manually.');
   180	  }
   181	
   182	  // Sub-call 2: session_health
   183	  let healthData: Record<string, unknown> = {};
   184	  try {
   185	    const healthResponse = await handleSessionHealth();
   186	    healthData = extractData(healthResponse);
   187	    allHints.push(...extractHints(healthData));
   188	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   189	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   190	    healthData = { error: message };
   191	    allHints.push('session_health failed. Try calling it manually.');
   192	  }
   193	
   194	  // Phase 027: Structural bootstrap contract
   195	  const structuralContext = buildStructuralBootstrapContract('session_bootstrap');
   196	  if (structuralContext.status === 'stale' || structuralContext.status === 'missing') {
   197	    allHints.push(
   198	      `Structural context is ${structuralContext.status}. Run code_graph_scan if needed, then re-run session_bootstrap.`
   199	    );
   200	  }
   201	
   202	  const cachedSummary = extractCachedSummary(resumeData);
   203	  if (cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted') {
   204	    allHints.push('Cached continuity summary accepted as additive bootstrap context.');
   205	  }
   206	  const structuralRoutingNudge = buildStructuralRoutingNudge(structuralContext);
   207	  if (structuralRoutingNudge) {
   208	    allHints.push(structuralRoutingNudge.message);
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  // Deduplicate hints
   212	  const uniqueHints = [...new Set(allHints)];
   213	
   214	  // Record bootstrap telemetry once for the composite call.
   215	  const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;
   216	  const completeness = resumeData.error || healthData.error ? 'partial' : 'full';
   217	  recordBootstrapEvent('tool', durationMs, completeness);
   218	
   219	  const resumeCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = resumeData.error ? 'unknown' : 'estimated';
   220	  const healthCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = healthData.error ? 'unknown' : 'estimated';
   221	  const cachedCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted' ? 'estimated' : 'defaulted';
   222	  const structuralCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = 'exact';
   223	  const nextActionsCertainty: SharedPayloadCertainty = 'defaulted';
   224	  const resumePayload = coerceSharedPayloadEnvelope(resumeData.payloadContract);
   225	  const healthPayload = coerceSharedPayloadEnvelope(healthData.payloadContract);
   226	  const structuralSnapshotTrust = buildStructuralContextTrust(structuralContext);
   227	  const resumeStructuralTrust = extractStructuralTrustFromPayload(resumePayload);
   228	  if (!resumeData.error && !resumeStructuralTrust) {
   229	    throw new StructuralTrustPayloadError(
   230	      'session_bootstrap expected session_resume to emit structural-context.structuralTrust.',
   231	    );
   232	  }
   233	  const structuralContextWithTrust = attachStructuralTrustFields(
   234	    structuralContext,
   235	    structuralSnapshotTrust,
   236	    { label: 'session_bootstrap structural context payload' },
   237	  );
   238	  const resumeWithTrust = resumeData.error
   239	    ? resumeData
   240	    : attachStructuralTrustFields(
   241	      resumeData,
   242	      resumeStructuralTrust,
   243	      { label: 'session_bootstrap resume payload' },
   244	    );
   245	
   246	  const payloadSections: SharedPayloadSection[] = [
   247	    {
   248	      key: 'resume-surface',
   249	      title: 'Resume Surface',
   250	      content: summarizeUnknown(resumeData),
   251	      source: 'memory',
   252	      certainty: resumeCertainty,
   253	    },
   254	  ];
   255	  if (cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted' && cachedSummary.cachedSummary) {
   256	    payloadSections.push({
   257	      key: 'cached-continuity',
   258	      title: 'Cached Continuity',
   259	      content: [
   260	        cachedSummary.cachedSummary.continuityText,
   261	        `Cache tokens: create=${cachedSummary.cachedSummary.cacheCreationInputTokens}; read=${cachedSummary.cachedSummary.cacheReadInputTokens}`,
   262	        `Transcript: ${cachedSummary.cachedSummary.transcriptFingerprint}`,
   263	      ].join(' | '),
   264	      source: 'session',
   265	      certainty: cachedCertainty,
   266	    });
   267	  }
   268	  payloadSections.push(
   269	    {
   270	      key: 'health-surface',
   271	      title: 'Health Surface',
   272	      content: summarizeUnknown(healthData),
   273	      source: 'operational',
   274	      certainty: healthCertainty,
   275	    },
   276	    {
   277	      key: 'structural-context',
   278	      title: 'Structural Context',
   279	      // This section carries trust derived from the local structural snapshot,
   280	      // which remains valid even when the remote resume surface fails closed.
   281	      content: structuralContext.summary,
   282	      source: 'code-graph',
   283	      certainty: structuralCertainty,
   284	      structuralTrust: structuralSnapshotTrust,
   285	    },
   286	    {
   287	      key: 'next-actions',
   288	      title: 'Next Actions',
   289	      content: buildNextActions(resumeData, healthData, structuralContext).join(' | '),
   290	      source: 'session',
   291	      certainty: nextActionsCertainty,
   292	    },
   293	  );
   294	
   295	  const payloadContract = createSharedPayloadEnvelope({
   296	    kind: 'bootstrap',
   297	    sections: payloadSections,
   298	    summary: `Bootstrap payload: ${summarizeCertaintyContract([
   299	      { label: 'resume', certainty: resumeCertainty },
   300	      { label: 'health', certainty: healthCertainty },
   301	      ...(cachedSummary?.status === 'accepted' ? [{ label: 'cached', certainty: cachedCertainty }] : []),
   302	      { label: 'structural', certainty: structuralCertainty },
   303	      { label: 'nextActions', certainty: nextActionsCertainty },
   304	    ])}; structuralStatus=${structuralContext.status}`,
   305	    provenance: {
   306	      producer: 'session_bootstrap',
   307	      sourceSurface: 'session_bootstrap',
   308	      trustState: trustStateFromStructuralStatus(structuralContext.status),
   309	      generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   310	      lastUpdated: structuralContext.provenance?.lastUpdated ?? null,
   311	      sourceRefs: ['session-resume', 'session-health', 'session-snapshot'],
   312	    },
   313	  });
   314	  const graphOps = buildCodeGraphOpsContract({
   315	    graphFreshness: structuralContext.status === 'ready'
   316	      ? 'fresh'
   317	      : structuralContext.status === 'stale'
   318	        ? 'stale'
   319	        : 'empty',
   320	    sourceSurface: 'session_bootstrap',
   321	  });
   322	
   323	  const result: SessionBootstrapResult = {
   324	    resume: resumeWithTrust,
   325	    health: healthData,
   326	    ...(cachedSummary ? { cachedSummary } : {}),
   327	    structuralContext: structuralContextWithTrust,
   328	    ...(structuralRoutingNudge ? { structuralRoutingNudge } : {}),
   329	    payloadContract,
   330	    opencodeTransport: buildOpenCodeTransportPlan({
   331	      bootstrapPayload: payloadContract,
   332	      resumePayload,
   333	      healthPayload,
   334	      specFolder: args.specFolder ?? null,
   335	    }),
   336	    graphOps,
   337	    hints: uniqueHints,
   338	    // Keep advisory routing guidance out of nextActions so bootstrap and resume
   339	    // remain the authoritative recovery owners for startup and deep resume flows.
   340	    nextActions: buildNextActions(resumeData, healthData, structuralContext),
   341	  };
   342	
   343	  return {
   344	    content: [{
   345	      type: 'text',
   346	      text: JSON.stringify({ status: 'ok', data: result }, null, 2),
   347	    }],
   348	    structuredContent: result,
   349	  };
   350	}

    67	/** Handle memory_delete tool -- deletes a single memory by ID or bulk-deletes by spec folder. */
    68	async function handleMemoryDelete(args: DeleteArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
    69	  await checkDatabaseUpdated();
    70	
    71	  const { id, specFolder, confirm } = args;
    72	  if (!id && !specFolder) throw new Error('Either id or specFolder is required');
    73	  if (specFolder !== undefined && typeof specFolder !== 'string') throw new Error('specFolder must be a string');
    74	  if (specFolder && !id && !confirm) throw new Error('Bulk delete requires confirm: true');
    75	
    76	  let numericId: number | null = null;
    77	  if (id !== undefined && id !== null) {
    78	    numericId = parseMemoryId(id);
    79	  }

    81	  let deletedCount = 0;
    82	  let checkpointName: string | null = null;
    83	  let mutationLedgerWarning: string | null = null;
    84	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
    85	
    86	  if (!database) {
    87	    // Unified DB-unavailable contract for single and bulk delete paths.
    88	    return createDatabaseUnavailableDeleteResponse();
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  if (numericId !== null) {
    92	    const singleSnapshot = getMemoryHashSnapshot(database, numericId);
    93	
    94	    // T2-5 transaction wrapper — wraps single-delete path (memory delete, causal edge
    95	    // Cleanup, ledger append) in a transaction for atomicity on error.
    96	    database.transaction(() => {
    97	      deletedCount = vectorIndex.deleteMemory(numericId) ? 1 : 0;
    98	
    99	      if (deletedCount > 0) {
   100	        // Record DELETE history after confirmed delete (no FK, history rows survive).
   101	        // Placed after deleteMemory to avoid false audit rows for non-existent IDs.
   102	        try {
   103	          recordHistory(
   104	            numericId,
   105	            'DELETE',
   106	            singleSnapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   107	            null,
   108	            'mcp:memory_delete',
   109	            singleSnapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   110	          );
   111	        } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   112	          // History recording is best-effort
   113	        }
   114	
   115	        causalEdges.init(database);
   116	        causalEdges.deleteEdgesForMemory(String(numericId));
   117	        // H1 FIX: Use db-specific invalidation instead of the no-op global version
   118	        clearDegreeCacheForDb(database);
   119	
   120	        const ledgerRecorded = appendMutationLedgerSafe(database, {
   121	          mutationType: 'delete',
   122	          reason: 'memory_delete: single memory delete',
   123	          priorHash: singleSnapshot?.content_hash ?? null,
   124	          newHash: mutationLedger.computeHash(`delete:${numericId}:${Date.now()}`),
   125	          linkedMemoryIds: [numericId],
   126	          decisionMeta: {
   127	            tool: 'memory_delete',
   128	            bulk: false,
   129	            memoryId: numericId,
   130	            specFolder: singleSnapshot?.spec_folder ?? null,
   131	            filePath: singleSnapshot?.file_path ?? null,
   132	          },
   133	          actor: 'mcp:memory_delete',
   134	        });
   135	        if (!ledgerRecorded) {
   136	          mutationLedgerWarning = 'Mutation ledger append failed; audit trail may be incomplete.';
   137	        }
   138	      }
   139	    })();
   140	  } else {
   141	    const memories: { id: number }[] = vectorIndex.getMemoriesByFolder(specFolder as string);
   142	    const deletedIds: number[] = [];
   143	    const hashById = new Map<number, MemoryHashSnapshot>();
   144	
   145	    if (database && 'prepare' in database) {
   146	      try {
   147	        const rows = database.prepare(`
   148	          SELECT id, content_hash, spec_folder, file_path
   149	          FROM memory_index
   150	          WHERE spec_folder = ?
   151	        `).all(specFolder as string) as MemoryHashSnapshot[];
   152	
   153	        for (const row of rows) {
   154	          hashById.set(row.id, row);
   155	        }
   156	      } catch (_err: unknown) {
   157	        // Non-fatal — bulk delete proceeds without per-memory hash snapshots; ledger entries will lack prior hashes.
   158	      }
   159	    }

   163	      const proposedCheckpointName = `pre-cleanup-${timestamp}`;
   164	      try {
   165	        const checkpoint = checkpoints.createCheckpoint({
   166	          name: proposedCheckpointName,
   167	          specFolder,
   168	          metadata: {
   169	            reason: 'auto-checkpoint before bulk delete',
   170	            memoryCount: memories.length,

    92	  let mutationLedgerWarning: string | null = null;

    94	  if (title !== undefined && title !== existing.title) {
    95	    console.error(`[memory-update] Title changed, regenerating embedding for memory ${id} [requestId=${requestId}]`);
    96	    let newEmbedding: Float32Array | null = null;
    97	
    98	    try {
    99	      // Embed title + content_text, not title alone.
   100	      // This produces better semantic embeddings that capture the full memory context.
   101	      const contentText = existing.content_text || '';
   102	      const embeddingInput = contentText ? `${title}\n\n${contentText}` : title;
   103	      newEmbedding = await embeddings.generateDocumentEmbedding(embeddingInput);
   104	    } catch (err: unknown) {
   105	      const message = toErrorMessage(err);
   106	      if (allowPartialUpdate) {
   107	        console.warn(`[memory-update] Embedding regeneration failed, marking for re-index [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   108	        embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = true;
   109	        embeddingMarkedForReindex = true;
   110	      } else {
   111	        console.error(`[memory-update] Embedding regeneration failed, rolling back update [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   112	        throw new MemoryError(
   113	          ErrorCodes.EMBEDDING_FAILED,
   114	          'Embedding regeneration failed, update rolled back. No changes were made.',
   115	          { originalError: message, memoryId: id }
   116	        );
   117	      }
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    if (newEmbedding) {
   121	      updateParams.embedding = newEmbedding;
   122	      embeddingRegenerated = true;
   123	    } else if (!embeddingMarkedForReindex) {
   124	      if (allowPartialUpdate) {
   125	        console.warn('[memory-update] Embedding returned null, marking for re-index');
   126	        embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite = true;
   127	        embeddingMarkedForReindex = true;
   128	      } else {
   129	        throw new MemoryError(
   130	          ErrorCodes.EMBEDDING_FAILED,
   131	          'Failed to regenerate embedding (null result), update rolled back. No changes were made.',
   132	          { memoryId: id }
   133	        );
   134	      }
   135	    }
   136	  }
   137	
   138	  // T2-5 transaction wrapper — wraps all synchronous mutation steps (DB update,
   139	  // Cache invalidation, BM25 re-index, ledger append) in a single transaction for atomicity.
   140	  // Embedding generation (async) runs before this block; its result feeds into updateParams.
   141	  const fields = Object.keys(updateParams).filter((key) => key !== 'id' && key !== 'embedding');
   142	
   143	  if (database) {
   144	    runInTransaction(database, () => {
   145	      if (embeddingStatusNeedsPendingWrite) {
   146	        vectorIndex.updateEmbeddingStatus(id, 'pending');
   147	      }
   148	
   149	      vectorIndex.updateMemory(updateParams);
   150	
   151	      // T2-6 — BM25 index stores title + trigger phrases; must re-index when either changes
   152	      // So keyword search reflects the updated content.
   153	      // T-05: BM25 re-index failure now rolls back the transaction when the index is operational.
   154	      // Infrastructure failures (BM25 not available, DB missing prepare) are non-fatal warnings.
   155	      if ((updateParams.title !== undefined || updateParams.triggerPhrases !== undefined) && bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {

   161	          if (row) {
   162	            const text = bm25Index.buildBm25DocumentText(row);
   163	            if (text.trim()) {
   164	              bm25Idx.addDocument(String(id), text);
   165	            }
   166	          }
   167	        } catch (e: unknown) {
   168	          const bm25ErrMsg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
   169	          // T-05 + P1-02 fix: Distinguish infrastructure failures from data failures.
   170	          // Infrastructure failures mean the BM25 subsystem is unavailable or torn down —
   171	          // These are non-fatal warnings. Data failures mean the index IS operational but rejected
   172	          // The input — those must re-throw to roll back the transaction.
   173	          const isBm25InfraFailure = (msg: string): boolean =>
   174	            msg.includes('not a function') ||
   175	            msg.includes('not initialized') ||
   176	            msg.includes('Cannot read properties') ||
   177	            msg.includes('is not defined') ||
   178	            msg.includes('database is closed') ||
   179	            msg.includes('no such table');
   180	          if (isBm25InfraFailure(bm25ErrMsg)) {
   181	            console.warn(`[memory-crud-update] BM25 infrastructure unavailable, skipping re-index [requestId=${requestId}]: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   182	          } else {
   183	            console.error(`[memory-crud-update] BM25 re-index failed, rolling back update [requestId=${requestId}]: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   184	            throw new Error(`BM25 re-index failed: ${bm25ErrMsg}`);
   185	          }
   186	        }
   187	      }
   188	
   189	      // T-05: Record UPDATE history after successful mutation
   190	      try {
   191	        recordHistory(

   193	          existing.title ?? null,

    70	  --stdin           Read structured JSON from stdin (preferred when a caller already has curated session data)

    85	Preferred save path (JSON-PRIMARY):
    86	  - ALWAYS use --stdin, --json, or a JSON temp file.
    87	  - The AI has strictly better information about its own session than any DB query can reconstruct.
    88	  - Explicit CLI targets still outrank payload specFolder values in every structured-input mode.
    89	
    90	Direct CLI target rule:
    91	  - When a spec folder is passed on the CLI, that explicit target is authoritative.
    92	  - Session learning, JSON SPEC_FOLDER fields, and auto-detect may inform logging,
    93	    but they must not reroute the save to another folder.

    97	    "user_prompts": [...],
    98	    "observations": [...],

   102	      { "tool": "Edit", "inputSummary": "Added deprecation warning", "outputSummary": "Inserted 10 lines", "status": "success" }
   103	    ],
   104	    "exchanges": [
   105	      { "userInput": "Implement the JSON-primary plan", "assistantResponse": "Updated 8 files...", "timestamp": "ISO-8601" }
   106	    ],
   107	    "preflight": {
   108	      "knowledgeScore": 40,
   109	      "uncertaintyScore": 60,
   110	      "contextScore": 50,
   111	      "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
   112	      "gaps": ["gap1", "gap2"],
   113	      "confidence": 45,
   114	      "readiness": "Needs research"
   115	    },
   116	    "postflight": {
   117	      "knowledgeScore": 75,
   118	      "uncertaintyScore": 25,
   119	      "contextScore": 80,
   120	      "gapsClosed": ["gap1"],
   121	      "newGaps": ["new-gap"]
   122	    }
   123	  }
   124	
   125	  Tool/Exchange enrichment fields (all optional — JSON-mode only):
   126	  - toolCalls[]: AI-summarized tool calls with tool name, input/output summaries, status, duration
   127	  - exchanges[]: Key user-assistant exchanges with timestamps
   128	  - These provide richer context than DB extraction since the AI filters noise at source
   129	
   130	  Learning Delta Calculation:
   131	  - Knowledge Delta = postflight.knowledgeScore - preflight.knowledgeScore
   132	  - Uncertainty Reduction = preflight.uncertaintyScore - postflight.uncertaintyScore
   133	  - Context Delta = postflight.contextScore - preflight.contextScore
   134	  - Learning Index = (Know x 0.4) + (Uncert x 0.35) + (Context x 0.25)
   135	`;

# Iteration 040: DEFINITIVE FINAL REPORT

## Focus
DEFINITIVE FINAL REPORT: Executive summary of 40 iterations. Top 10 recommendations ranked by impact x feasibility. Implementation priority order. One-page decision brief.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Add a transport-owned compaction memory action card
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:210](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L210), [opencode-transport.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts#L140)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects explicit memory-tool behavior during compaction; Public already injects continuity payloads but not equally direct memory-action guidance.
- **Why it matters**: This is the cleanest transfer with the best payoff and lowest architectural risk.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: Reuse Mnemosyne’s memory discipline, but translate it into Public authority surfaces
- **Source**: [external/README.md:62](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L62), [context-server.ts:755](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L755), [session-bootstrap.ts:163](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L163)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne repeats “recall early, save after decisions, delete contradictions”; Public already has richer recovery and governance.
- **Why it matters**: The pattern is good, but the verbs must be `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, governed save flows, and careful revision, not raw add/delete habits.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 3: Prototype read-only `project` and `global` recall aliases over existing scope logic
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:118](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L118), [memory-search.ts:529](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L529), [memory-search.ts:818](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L818)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne offers simple project/global recall tools; Public already has `retrievalLevel` and scoped search routing.
- **Why it matters**: Ergonomics can improve, but only as aliases that compile into current governed retrieval.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Prototype optional offline-backend readiness, not an offline-first default swap
- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary and first-run model download; Public already exposes readiness and recovery hints.
- **Why it matters**: If Public ever supports optional local backends, it needs explicit doctor/warmup/readiness UX, not hidden dependency failure.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 5: A `memory_remember` helper is valid only as a governed facade
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [generate-context.js:85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [memory-save.ts:1128](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1128)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne supports direct string saves; Public requires file-backed, governed, preflighted ingest.
- **Why it matters**: A convenience surface is plausible only if it emits the same JSON-primary save path and governance checks.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 6: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:323](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L323)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne proves a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel ergonomic; Public already emits OpenCode transport from MCP-owned state.
- **Why it matters**: A facade could improve DX, but it must delegate to MCP and preserve separate memory, CocoIndex, and code-graph authorities.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 7: Do not re-platform Public’s retrieval core around Mnemosyne
- **Source**: [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [memory-search.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [hybrid-search.ts:4](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L4)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne documents hybrid retrieval; Public already implements visible vector/FTS/BM25/RRF-style hybrid search in-process.
- **Why it matters**: Mnemosyne is an ergonomics reference, not a stronger retrieval engine for this codebase.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 8: Do not import basename-derived project identity as Public memory authority
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:27](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L27), [external/src/index.ts:78](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L78), [memory-search.ts:533](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L533)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne turns directory basename into collection identity and auto-inits from it.
- **Why it matters**: That collides with Public’s governed tenant/user/agent/session/shared-space model and is too fragile across renames and worktrees.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 9: Do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut to Public’s main memory surface
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/README.md:57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L57), [memory-search.ts:507](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L507)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a single tag.
- **Why it matters**: Public already has explicit constitutional behavior; a shortcut would blur durable rules with ordinary memories and invite over-promotion.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 10: Do not adopt Mnemosyne’s append-plus-hard-delete model as Public’s primary mutation contract
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes plain add/delete behavior; Public wraps updates and deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, and recovery-safe invalidation.
- **Why it matters**: Public should keep revision and auditability as first-class mutation rules.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

## One-Page Decision Brief
Mnemosyne’s value to Public is behavioral and ergonomic, not architectural. After 40 iterations, the final call is: keep Public’s existing hybrid retrieval, governed scope, and audited mutation model; borrow only the thin, high-signal operator cues.

The two moves worth doing now are:
1. add a deduped compaction-time memory action card on the OpenCode transport surface;
2. rewrite startup and AGENTS-style guidance in Public terms around `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, and governed save/update/delete flows.

The medium-priority exploration track is bounded:
1. read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases over existing scope logic;
2. a governed `memory_remember` convenience facade;
3. a delegation-only OpenCode memory plugin facade;
4. optional offline-backend readiness tooling.

The non-goals are definitive:
1. no retrieval-core replatforming around Mnemosyne;
2. no basename-derived scope authority;
3. no `core=true` shortcut on the main surface;
4. no blind append/hard-delete mutation model.

## Sources Consulted
- [external/src/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts)
- [external/package.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json)
- [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md)
- [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts)
- [hybrid-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts)
- [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
- [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
- [opencode-transport.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/context/opencode-transport.ts)

- [iteration-024.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md#L1807)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: `0.05`
- Questions addressed: `[final recommendation ranking, implementation ordering, stable non-goals, wrapper-vs-Public authority boundary]`
- Questions answered: `[Mnemosyne is an ergonomics reference, not a backend replacement; the best transfer is compaction/startup guidance; any new facade must delegate to existing MCP/governance authority; scope/mutation shortcuts should be rejected]`
- Novelty justification: `This pass adds almost no new mechanisms; it converts the 40-iteration convergence into a definitive implementation order and explicit non-goals.`
- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, with the known read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

## Ruled Out
- Replacing Public’s primary recovery contract with `memory_recall`-style startup semantics.
- Replatforming Public’s hybrid retrieval stack around Mnemosyne’s wrapper/backend contract.
- Importing basename-derived `project/global` identity as Public scope authority.
- Adding `core=true` or blind append/delete as first-class Public mutation shortcuts.

## Reflection
- What worked: source-first comparison of Mnemosyne’s thin wrapper against Public’s actual recovery, transport, retrieval, and governed mutation paths.
- What did not work: the external repo still proves very little about backend internals, so definitive conclusions come from boundary analysis, not backend reverse-engineering.
- What I would do differently: the next phase should stop researching and move straight into an implementation packet for the top two adopt-now items plus a separate facade-spec packet.

## Recommended Next Focus
Open implementation work, not more research:
1. transport-owned compaction memory action card;
2. Public-native startup/AGENTS wording update;
3. packetized design for governed `memory_remember` plus optional read-only recall aliases.

Read-only note: I did not update [research/research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/research.md), phase checklists, `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js`, because this sandbox is read-only.

### Finding 4: Prototype optional offline-backend readiness, not an offline-first default swap
- **Source**: [external/README.md:18](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L18), [external/src/index.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L67), [context-server.ts:751](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts#L751)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne assumes a local binary and first-run model download; Public already exposes readiness and recovery hints.
- **Why it matters**: If Public ever supports optional local backends, it needs explicit doctor/warmup/readiness UX, not hidden dependency failure.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 5: A `memory_remember` helper is valid only as a governed facade
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L138), [generate-context.js:85](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js#L85), [memory-save.ts:1128](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts#L1128)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne supports direct string saves; Public requires file-backed, governed, preflighted ingest.
- **Why it matters**: A convenience surface is plausible only if it emits the same JSON-primary save path and governance checks.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 6: A native OpenCode memory plugin is viable only as a delegation-only facade
- **Source**: [external/package.json:31](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json#L31), [external/src/index.ts:38](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L38), [session-bootstrap.ts:323](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts#L323)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne proves a thin OpenCode wrapper can feel ergonomic; Public already emits OpenCode transport from MCP-owned state.
- **Why it matters**: A facade could improve DX, but it must delegate to MCP and preserve separate memory, CocoIndex, and code-graph authorities.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 7: Do not re-platform Public’s retrieval core around Mnemosyne
- **Source**: [external/README.md:82](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L82), [memory-search.ts:482](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [hybrid-search.ts:4](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L4)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne documents hybrid retrieval; Public already implements visible vector/FTS/BM25/RRF-style hybrid search in-process.
- **Why it matters**: Mnemosyne is an ergonomics reference, not a stronger retrieval engine for this codebase.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 8: Do not import basename-derived project identity as Public memory authority
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:27](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L27), [external/src/index.ts:78](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L78), [memory-search.ts:533](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L533)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne turns directory basename into collection identity and auto-inits from it.
- **Why it matters**: That collides with Public’s governed tenant/user/agent/session/shared-space model and is too fragile across renames and worktrees.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 9: Do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut to Public’s main memory surface
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:140](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L140), [external/README.md:57](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md#L57), [memory-search.ts:507](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L507)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne collapses “always-on” behavior into a single tag.
- **Why it matters**: Public already has explicit constitutional behavior; a shortcut would blur durable rules with ordinary memories and invite over-promotion.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: medium; **Source strength**: both

### Finding 10: Do not adopt Mnemosyne’s append-plus-hard-delete model as Public’s primary mutation contract
- **Source**: [external/src/index.ts:147](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L147), [external/src/index.ts:193](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts#L193), [memory-crud-delete.ts:67](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-delete.ts#L67), [memory-crud-update.ts:138](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-update.ts#L138)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne exposes plain add/delete behavior; Public wraps updates and deletes in transactions, history, ledgering, and recovery-safe invalidation.
- **Why it matters**: Public should keep revision and auditability as first-class mutation rules.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high; **Source strength**: primary

## One-Page Decision Brief
Mnemosyne’s value to Public is behavioral and ergonomic, not architectural. After 40 iterations, the final call is: keep Public’s existing hybrid retrieval, governed scope, and audited mutation model; borrow only the thin, high-signal operator cues.

The two moves worth doing now are:
1. add a deduped compaction-time memory action card on the OpenCode transport surface;
2. rewrite startup and AGENTS-style guidance in Public terms around `session_bootstrap`, `memory_context`, and governed save/update/delete flows.

The medium-priority exploration track is bounded:
1. read-only `project` / `global` recall aliases over existing scope logic;
2. a governed `memory_remember` convenience facade;
3. a delegation-only OpenCode memory plugin facade;
4. optional offline-backend readiness tooling.

The non-goals are definitive:
1. no retrieval-core replatforming around Mnemosyne;
2. no basename-derived scope authority;
3. no `core=true` shortcut on the main surface;
4. no blind append/hard-delete mutation model.

## Assessment
- New information ratio: `0.05`
- Questions addressed: `[final recommendation ranking, implementation ordering, stable non-goals, wrapper-vs-Public authority boundary]`
- Questions answered: `[Mnemosyne is an ergonomics reference, not a backend replacement; the best transfer is compaction/startup guidance; any new facade must delegate to existing MCP/governance authority; scope/mutation shortcuts should be rejected]`
- Novelty justification: `This pass adds almost no new mechanisms; it converts the 40-iteration convergence into a definitive implementation order and explicit non-goals.`
- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `Summary: Errors: 0  Warnings: 0` and `RESULT: PASSED`, with the known read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

## Ruled Out
- Replacing Public’s primary recovery contract with `memory_recall`-style startup semantics.
- Replatforming Public’s hybrid retrieval stack around Mnemosyne’s wrapper/backend contract.
- Importing basename-derived `project/global` identity as Public scope authority.
- Adding `core=true` or blind append/delete as first-class Public mutation shortcuts.

## Reflection
- What worked: source-first comparison of Mnemosyne’s thin wrapper against Public’s actual recovery, transport, retrieval, and governed mutation paths.
- What did not work: the external repo still proves very little about backend internals, so definitive conclusions come from boundary analysis, not backend reverse-engineering.
- What I would do differently: the next phase should stop researching and move straight into an implementation packet for the top two adopt-now items plus a separate facade-spec packet.

## Recommended Next Focus
Open implementation work, not more research:
1. transport-owned compaction memory action card;
2. Public-native startup/AGENTS wording update;
3. packetized design for governed `memory_remember` plus optional read-only recall aliases.
